# Integration Checklist: LSP Client Integration

**Purpose**: Validate SPEC-008 integration requirements for local language-server configuration, lifecycle handling, prerequisite validation, and parity ownership.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are command-bearing registry rows specified for TypeScript/JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, C/C++, Swift, and Java? [Completeness, Spec §FR-026, Plan §Language Parity Matrix]
- [ ] CHK002 Are baseline registry or disposition rows specified for C#, Kotlin, PHP, Ruby, Dart, Vue, and COBOL? [Completeness, Spec §FR-026-FR-027, Plan §Language Parity Matrix]
- [ ] CHK003 Are all internal baseline capability rows mapped to implementation evidence or concrete numbered future-spec ownership? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014-FR-015, Plan §Feature and Capability Parity Matrix]
- [ ] CHK004 Are project configuration, environment override, and activation controls all specified without giving environment variables activation power? [Completeness, Spec §FR-032-FR-034, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [x] CHK005 Is command/PATH probing behavior specified for registry defaults, configured argv, accepted alternatives, resolved executable paths, and unavailable-command reporting? [Completeness, Spec §FR-036-FR-037, Plan §Prerequisite and Version Evidence Policy]
- [ ] CHK006 Are JSON-RPC initialize, request, timeout, and shutdown requirements specified for the local stdio session lifecycle? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Validation Strategy, Data Model §LspWorkspaceSession]
- [x] CHK007 Does the spec define restart or retry behavior after a server crash or timeout during runtime and watch verification? [Coverage, Spec §FR-038, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK008 Is timeout precedence fully ordered across per-language environment, project language, global environment, project default, and registry default values? [Clarity, Spec §Configuration Contract, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [ ] CHK009 Is activation precedence unambiguous, including explicit CLI disable overriding project configuration? [Clarity, Spec §FR-034, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "unique target" defined with Location/LocationLink normalization, deduplication, and CodeGraph node compatibility? [Clarity, Spec §FR-029-FR-030]
- [ ] CHK011 Are normal runtime degradation and strict SPEC-008 validation failure semantics clearly separated? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009-FR-011, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK012 Are accepted language-server alternatives specific enough for implementers while avoiding exact version pins? [Clarity, Spec §Server Prerequisite Matrix, Plan §Language Parity Matrix]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK013 Do the no auto-install, no auto-enable, local subprocess-only, and no remote-service constraints align across spec, plan, and contracts? [Consistency, Spec §FR-020-FR-024, Plan §Constraints]
- [ ] CHK014 Are command alternatives consistent between the spec prerequisite matrix, plan language matrix, and validation quickstart? [Consistency, Spec §Server Prerequisite Matrix, Plan §Language Parity Matrix]
- [ ] CHK015 Are owned, future-owned, and disposition rows consistent with the no-unowned parity rule? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012-FR-015, Plan §Language Parity Matrix, Plan §Feature and Capability Parity Matrix]
- [ ] CHK016 Are SPEC-009 LSP-server facade and SPEC-010 rename/refactor exclusions consistently treated as non-goals? [Consistency, Spec §FR-022-FR-023, Plan §Constraints]
- [ ] CHK017 Are spec artifacts free of outbound links and external baseline project names while preserving the internal baseline rows? [Consistency, Internal baseline §Closure Rule]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK018 Are missing, unavailable-through-config, crashed, timed-out, malformed, ambiguous, external, generated, and unindexed response scenarios addressed in requirements? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [ ] CHK019 Are incremental watch requirements bounded to the existing watcher, post-resolution work, changed-file sets, and recorded skip reasons? [Coverage, Spec §FR-028, Spec §FR-035, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [ ] CHK020 Are no-files-present language scenarios specified as not-present rather than failure cases? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §LanguageCoverageRecord]
- [ ] CHK021 Are edge correction and suppression scenarios specified without duplicate active edges or speculative replacements? [Coverage, Spec §FR-018-FR-019, Spec §FR-029-FR-030]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK022 Are success criteria measurable for default-off behavior, per-language coverage, prereq failures, parity failures, correction metadata, ambiguity handling, and final evidence? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001-SC-009]
- [ ] CHK023 Is final validation evidence specified enough to prove real-server coverage for every SPEC-008-owned language row? [Acceptance Criteria, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [ ] CHK024 Are self-repo dogfood requirements defined with LSP explicitly enabled and non-LSP behavior preserved? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-009, Plan §Validation Strategy]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK025 Are local language-server prerequisites, observed-version evidence, and minimum-runtime evidence documented without auto-install or exact version pins? [Dependency, Spec §Server Prerequisite Matrix, Plan §Prerequisite and Version Evidence Policy]
- [ ] CHK026 Are invalid project config and environment override values required to warn and fall back to lower-precedence values? [Dependency, Spec §FR-033, Data Model §LspProjectConfig, Data Model §LspEnvironmentOverride]
- [ ] CHK027 Is the registry/prereq/status source of truth specified clearly enough to avoid drift across CLI, status, validation, and watch paths? [Consistency, Plan §Project Structure, Research §Decision: Add an explicit server registry and prereq checker]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: integration registry coverage, local subprocess lifecycle, configuration and timeout precedence, prerequisite validation, and parity ownership.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer checklist for Phase 4 planning readiness.
- Explicit must-have items incorporated: full language-server registry, baseline dispositions, internal feature/capability baseline, PATH probing, `codegraph.json`, environment overrides, timeout precedence, JSON-RPC lifecycle, crash/restart behavior, real-server prereqs, missing-binary messages, no auto-install, no remote service dependency, and no unowned parity rows.
- Remediation rerun: CHK005, CHK007, and CHK008 were re-evaluated after spec/plan updates on 2026-07-05; no integration gaps remain.
